name: Salmon and Cucumber Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut cucumbers into thin julienne strips after peeling.
2. First, cut the cucumber in half length-wise and scoop out the seeds with a teaspoon.
3. Then, cut the halves into quarters and quarters into crosswise strips about two inches long.
4. Next, cut these pieces into matchstick-size julienne.
5. In a frying pan, heat 3 tablespoons of butter until bubbling.
6. Add cucumbers and toss over moderately high heat for 2-3 minutes.
7. Season to taste with salt, sugar, vinegar, and pepper if desired.
8. Remove from the pan, stack strips, and cut crosswise into fine dice.
9. Toss with remaining seasoning (vinegar, salt, sugar).
10. Fold in 1 cup sour cream and a sprinkle of minced dill or watercress.
11. Place salmon fillets over 2 quarts simmering water mixed with 1 tablespoon salt and 1/3 cup wine vinegar.
12. Steam for 9-10 minutes.
13. Remove salmon, cover, and let cool for 20 minutes before chilling.
14. Discard poaching broth.
15. Arrange fillets on a platter with watercress or crisp salad greens.
16. Spoon the cucumber sauce over them and serve with baked tomatoes, oven-browned potatoes, and light rye bread.
17. Serves 4.
